# Kubernetes Controller Runtime Reconciler Registration

## Your Task

Trace how a controller registers itself with the controller-runtime Manager in kubernetes/kubernetes. Find the Go source files that define the Reconciler interface, implement SetupWithManager, and wire the controller into the manager's reconcile loop.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossrepo tracing domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle â€” completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you trace how a controller registers itself with the controller-runtime Manager. Let me start by exploring the workspace structure and searching for the relevant files.

1â†’/*
     2â†’Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.
     3â†’
     4â†’Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5â†’you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6â†’You may obtain a copy of the License at
     7â†’
     8â†’    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9â†’
    10â†’Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11â†’distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12â†’WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13â†’See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14â†’limitations under the License.
    15â†’*/
    16â†’
    17â†’// Package reconciler implements interfaces that attempt to reconcile the
    18â†’// desired state of the with the actual state of the world by triggering
    19â†’// actions.
    20â†’package reconciler
    21â†’
    22â†’import (
    23â†’	"context"
    24â†’	"fmt"
    25â†’	"strings"
    26â†’	"time"
    27â†’
    28â†’	v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    29â†’	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
    30â†’	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    31â†’	corelisters "k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1"
    32â†’	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
    33â†’	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    34â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/volume/attachdetach/cache"
    35â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/volume/attachdetach/metrics"
    36â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/volume/attachdetach/statusupdater"
    37â†’	kevents "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/events"
    38â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/goroutinemap/exponentialbackoff"
    39â†’	nodeutil "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/node"
    40â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/taints"
    41â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume/util"
    42â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume/util/operationexecutor"
    43â†’)
    44â†’
    45â†’// Reconciler runs a periodic loop to reconcile the desired state of the world with
    46â†’// the actual state of the world by triggering attach detach operations.
    47â†’// Note: This is distinct from the Reconciler implemented by the kubelet volume
    48â†’// manager. This reconciles state for the attach/detach controller. That
    49â†’// reconciles state for the kubelet volume manager.
    50â†’type Reconciler interface {
    51â†’	// Starts running the reconciliation loop which executes periodically, checks
    52â†’	// if volumes that should be attached are attached and volumes that should
    53â†’	// be detached are detached. If not, it will trigger attach/detach
    54â†’	// operations to rectify.
    55â†’	Run(ctx context.Context)
    56â†’}
    57â†’
    58â†’// NewReconciler returns a new instance of Reconciler that waits loopPeriod
    59â†’// between successive executions.
    60â†’// loopPeriod is the amount of time the reconciler loop waits between
    61â†’// successive executions.
    62â†’// maxWaitForUnmountDuration is the max amount of time the reconciler will wait
    63â†’// for the volume to be safely unmounted, after this it will detach the volume
    64â†’// anyway (to handle crashed/unavailable nodes). If during this time the volume
    65â†’// becomes used by a new pod, the detach request will be aborted and the timer
    66â†’// cleared.
    67â†’func NewReconciler(
    68â†’	loopPeriod time.Duration,
    69â†’	maxWaitForUnmountDuration time.Duration,
    70â†’	syncDuration time.Duration,
    71â†’	disableReconciliationSync bool,
    72â†’	disableForceDetachOnTimeout bool,
    73â†’	desiredStateOfWorld cache.DesiredStateOfWorld,
    74â†’	actualStateOfWorld cache.ActualStateOfWorld,
    75â†’	attacherDetacher operationexecutor.OperationExecutor,
    76â†’	nodeStatusUpdater statusupdater.NodeStatusUpdater,
    77â†’	nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister,
    78â†’	recorder record.EventRecorder) Reconciler {
    79â†’	return &reconciler{
    80â†’		loopPeriod:                  loopPeriod,
    81â†’		maxWaitForUnmountDuration:   maxWaitForUnmountDuration,
    82â†’		syncDuration:                syncDuration,
    83â†’		disableReconciliationSync:   disableReconciliationSync,
    84â†’		disableForceDetachOnTimeout: disableForceDetachOnTimeout,
    85â†’		desiredStateOfWorld:         desiredStateOfWorld,
    86â†’		actualStateOfWorld:          actualStateOfWorld,
    87â†’		attacherDetacher:            attacherDetacher,
    88â†’		nodeStatusUpdater:           nodeStatusUpdater,
    89â†’		nodeLister:                  nodeLister,
    90â†’		timeOfLastSync:              time.Now(),
    91â†’		recorder:                    recorder,
    92â†’	}
    93â†’}
    94â†’
    95â†’type reconciler struct {
    96â†’	loopPeriod                  time.Duration
    97â†’	maxWaitForUnmountDuration   time.Duration
    98â†’	syncDuration                time.Duration
    99â†’	desiredStateOfWorld         cache.DesiredStateOfWorld
   100â†’	actualStateOfWorld          cache.ActualStateOfWorld
   101â†’	attacherDetacher            operationexecutor.OperationExecutor
   102â†’	nodeStatusUpdater           statusupdater.NodeStatusUpdater
   103â†’	nodeLister                  corelisters.NodeLister
   104â†’	timeOfLastSync              time.Time
   105â†’	disableReconciliationSync   bool
   106â†’	disableForceDetachOnTimeout bool
   107â†’	recorder                    record.EventRecorder
   108â†’}
   109â†’
   110â†’func (rc *reconciler) Run(ctx context.Context) {
   111â†’	wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, rc.reconciliationLoopFunc(ctx), rc.loopPeriod)
   112â†’}
   113â†’
   114â†’// reconciliationLoopFunc this can be disabled via cli option disableReconciliation.
   115â†’// It periodically checks whether the attached volumes from actual state
   116â†’// are still attached to the node and update the status if they are not.
   117â†’func (rc *reconciler) reconciliationLoopFunc(ctx context.Context) func(context.Context) {
   118â†’	return func(ctx context.Context) {
   119â†’
   120â†’		rc.reconcile(ctx)
   121â†’		logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
   122â†’		if rc.disableReconciliationSync {
   123â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Skipping reconciling attached volumes still attached since it is disabled via the command line")
   124â†’		} else if rc.syncDuration < time.Second {
   125â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Skipping reconciling attached volumes still attached since it is set to less than one second via the command line")
   126â†’		} else if time.Since(rc.timeOfLastSync) > rc.syncDuration {
   127â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Starting reconciling attached volumes still attached")
   128â†’			rc.sync()
   129â†’		}
   130â†’	}
   131â†’}
   132â†’
   133â†’func (rc *reconciler) sync() {
   134â†’	defer rc.updateSyncTime()
   135â†’	rc.syncStates()
   136â†’}
   137â†’
   138â†’func (rc *reconciler) updateSyncTime() {
   139â†’	rc.timeOfLastSync = time.Now()
   140â†’}
   141â†’
   142â†’func (rc *reconciler) syncStates() {
   143â†’	volumesPerNode := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachedVolumesPerNode()
   144â†’	rc.attacherDetacher.VerifyVolumesAreAttached(volumesPerNode, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   145â†’}
   146â†’
   147â†’// hasOutOfServiceTaint returns true if the node has out-of-service taint present.
   148â†’func (rc *reconciler) hasOutOfServiceTaint(nodeName types.NodeName) (bool, error) {
   149â†’	node, err := rc.nodeLister.Get(string(nodeName))
   150â†’	if err != nil {
   151â†’		return false, err
   152â†’	}
   153â†’	return taints.TaintKeyExists(node.Spec.Taints, v1.TaintNodeOutOfService), nil
   154â†’}
   155â†’
   156â†’// nodeIsHealthy returns true if the node looks healthy.
   157â†’func (rc *reconciler) nodeIsHealthy(nodeName types.NodeName) (bool, error) {
   158â†’	node, err := rc.nodeLister.Get(string(nodeName))
   159â†’	if err != nil {
   160â†’		return false, err
   161â†’	}
   162â†’	return nodeutil.IsNodeReady(node), nil
   163â†’}
   164â†’
   165â†’func (rc *reconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context) {
   166â†’	// Detaches are triggered before attaches so that volumes referenced by
   167â†’	// pods that are rescheduled to a different node are detached first.
   168â†’
   169â†’	// Ensure volumes that should be detached are detached.
   170â†’	logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
   171â†’	for _, attachedVolume := range rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachedVolumes() {
   172â†’		if !rc.desiredStateOfWorld.VolumeExists(
   173â†’			attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName) {
   174â†’
   175â†’			// Check whether there already exist an operation pending, and don't even
   176â†’			// try to start an operation if there is already one running.
   177â†’			// This check must be done before we do any other checks, as otherwise the other checks
   178â†’			// may pass while at the same time the volume leaves the pending state, resulting in
   179â†’			// double detach attempts
   180â†’			// The operation key format is different depending on whether the volume
   181â†’			// allows multi attach across different nodes.
   182â†’			if util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(attachedVolume.VolumeSpec) {
   183â†’				if !rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationSafeToRetry(attachedVolume.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, attachedVolume.NodeName, operationexecutor.DetachOperationName) {
   184â†’					logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running or still in exponential backoff for node. Can't start detach", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   185â†’					continue
   186â†’				}
   187â†’			} else {
   188â†’				if !rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationSafeToRetry(attachedVolume.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, "" /* nodeName */, operationexecutor.DetachOperationName) {
   189â†’					logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running or still in exponential backoff in the cluster. Can't start detach for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   190â†’					continue
   191â†’				}
   192â†’			}
   193â†’
   194â†’			// Because the detach operation updates the ActualStateOfWorld before
   195â†’			// marking itself complete, it's possible for the volume to be removed
   196â†’			// from the ActualStateOfWorld between the GetAttachedVolumes() check
   197â†’			// and the IsOperationSafeToRetry() check above.
   198â†’			// Check the ActualStateOfWorld again to avoid issuing an unnecessary
   199â†’			// detach.
   200â†’			// See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/93902
   201â†’			attachState := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachState(attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   202â†’			if attachState == cache.AttachStateDetached {
   203â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Volume detached--skipping", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   204â†’				continue
   205â†’			}
   206â†’
   207â†’			// Set the detach request time
   208â†’			elapsedTime, err := rc.actualStateOfWorld.SetDetachRequestTime(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   209â†’			if err != nil {
   210â†’				logger.Error(err, "Cannot trigger detach because it fails to set detach request time with error")
   211â†’				continue
   212â†’			}
   213â†’			// Check whether the umount drain timer expired
   214â†’			maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired := elapsedTime > rc.maxWaitForUnmountDuration
   215â†’
   216â†’			isHealthy, err := rc.nodeIsHealthy(attachedVolume.NodeName)
   217â†’			if err != nil {
   218â†’				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get health of node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   219â†’			}
   220â†’
   221â†’			// Force detach volumes from unhealthy nodes after maxWaitForUnmountDuration if force detach is enabled
   222â†’			// Ensure that the timeout condition checks this correctly so that the correct metric is updated below
   223â†’			forceDetatchTimeoutExpired := maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && !rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout
   224â†’			if maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout {
   225â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Drain timeout expired for volume but disableForceDetachOnTimeout was set", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   226â†’			}
   227â†’			forceDetach := !isHealthy && forceDetatchTimeoutExpired
   228â†’
   229â†’			hasOutOfServiceTaint, err := rc.hasOutOfServiceTaint(attachedVolume.NodeName)
   230â†’			if err != nil {
   231â†’				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get taint specs for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   232â†’			}
   233â†’
   234â†’			// Check whether volume is still mounted. Skip detach if it is still mounted unless we have
   235â†’			// decided to force detach or the node has `node.kubernetes.io/out-of-service` taint.
   236â†’			if attachedVolume.MountedByNode && !forceDetach && !hasOutOfServiceTaint {
   237â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Cannot detach volume because it is still mounted", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   238â†’				continue
   239â†’			}
   240â†’
   241â†’			// Before triggering volume detach, mark volume as detached and update the node status
   242â†’			// If it fails to update node status, skip detach volume
   243â†’			// If volume detach operation fails, the volume needs to be added back to report as attached so that node status
   244â†’			// has the correct volume attachment information.
   245â†’			err = rc.actualStateOfWorld.RemoveVolumeFromReportAsAttached(attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   246â†’			if err != nil {
   247â†’				logger.V(5).Info("RemoveVolumeFromReportAsAttached failed while removing volume from node",
   248â†’					"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),

   282â†’							"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)

   315â†’func (rc *reconciler) attachDesiredVolumes(logger klog.Logger) {
   316â†’	// Ensure volumes that should be attached are attached.
   317â†’	for _, volumeToAttach := range rc.desiredStateOfWorld.GetVolumesToAttach() {
   318â†’		if util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(volumeToAttach.VolumeSpec) {
   319â†’			// Don't even try to start an operation if there is already one running for the given volume and node.
   320â†’			if rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationPending(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, volumeToAttach.NodeName) {

   323â†’			}
   324â†’		} else {
   325â†’			// Don't even try to start an operation if there is already one running for the given volume
   326â†’			if rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationPending(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, "" /* nodeName */) {
   327â†’				logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running. Can't start attach for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(volumeToAttach.NodeName)), "volumeNames", volumeToAttach.VolumeName)

   329â†’			}
   330â†’		}
   331â†’
   332â†’		// Because the attach operation updates the ActualStateOfWorld before
   333â†’		// marking itself complete, IsOperationPending() must be checked before
   334â†’		// GetAttachState() to guarantee the ActualStateOfWorld is
   335â†’		// up-to-date when it's read.

   337â†’		attachState := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachState(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)

   339â†’			// Volume/Node exists, touch it to reset detachRequestedTime
   340â†’			logger.V(10).Info("Volume attached--touching", "volume", volumeToAttach)
   341â†’			rc.actualStateOfWorld.ResetDetachRequestTime(logger, volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   342â†’			continue
   343â†’		}
   344â†’
   345â†’		if !util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(volumeToAttach.VolumeSpec) {
   346â†’			nodes := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetNodesForAttachedVolume(volumeToAttach.VolumeName)

   348â†’				if !volumeToAttach.MultiAttachErrorReported {
   349â†’					rc.reportMultiAttachError(logger, volumeToAttach, nodes)
   350â†’					rc.desiredStateOfWorld.SetMultiAttachError(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   351â†’				}

   353â†’			}
   354â†’		}

   356â†’		// Volume/Node doesn't exist, spawn a goroutine to attach it

   358â†’		err := rc.attacherDetacher.AttachVolume(logger, volumeToAttach.VolumeToAttach, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   359â†’		if err == nil {
   360â†’			logger.Info("attacherDetacher.AttachVolume started", "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "nodeName", volumeToAttach.NodeName, "scheduledPods", klog.KObjSlice(volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods))
   361â†’		}
   362â†’		if err != nil && !exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff(err) {
   363â†’			// Ignore exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff errors, they are expected.
   364â†’			// Log all other errors.
   365â†’			logger.Error(err, "attacherDetacher.AttachVolume failed to start", "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "nodeName", volumeToAttach.NodeName, "scheduledPods", klog.KObjSlice(volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods))

   367â†’	}

   370â†’// reportMultiAttachError sends events and logs situation that a volume that
   371â†’// should be attached to a node is already attached to different node(s).
   372â†’func (rc *reconciler) reportMultiAttachError(logger klog.Logger, volumeToAttach cache.VolumeToAttach, nodes []types.NodeName) {
   373â†’	// Filter out the current node from list of nodes where the volume is
   374â†’	// attached.
   375â†’	// Some methods need []string, some other needs []NodeName, collect both.
   376â†’	// In theory, these arrays should have always only one element - the
   377â†’	// controller does not allow more than one attachment. But use array just
   378â†’	// in case...
   379â†’	otherNodes := []types.NodeName{}
   380â†’	otherNodesStr := []string{}
   381â†’	for _, node := range nodes {
   382â†’		if node != volumeToAttach.NodeName {
   383â†’			otherNodes = append(otherNodes, node)
   384â†’			otherNodesStr = append(otherNodesStr, string(node))
   385â†’		}
   386â†’	}
   387â†’
   388â†’	// Get list of pods that use the volume on the other nodes.
   389â†’	pods := rc.desiredStateOfWorld.GetVolumePodsOnNodes(otherNodes, volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   390â†’	if len(pods) == 0 {
   391â†’		// We did not find any pods that requests the volume. The pod must have been deleted already.
   392â†’		simpleMsg, _ := volumeToAttach.GenerateMsg("Multi-Attach error", "Volume is already exclusively attached to one node and can't be attached to another")
   393â†’		for _, pod := range volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods {
   394â†’			rc.recorder.Eventf(pod, v1.EventTypeWarning, kevents.FailedAttachVolume, simpleMsg)
   395â†’		}
   396â†’		// Log detailed message to system admin
   397â†’		logger.Info("Multi-Attach error: volume is already exclusively attached and can't be attached to another node", "attachedTo", otherNodesStr, "volume", volumeToAttach)
   398â†’		return
   399â†’	}
   400â†’
   401â†’	// There are pods that require the volume and run on another node. Typically
   402â†’	// it's user error, e.g. a ReplicaSet uses a PVC and has >1 replicas. Let
   403â†’	// the user know what pods are blocking the volume.
   404â†’	for _, scheduledPod := range volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods {
   405â†’		// Each scheduledPod must get a custom message. They can run in
   406â†’		// different namespaces and user of a namespace should not see names of
   407â†’		// pods in other namespaces.
   408â†’		localPodNames := []string{} // Names of pods in scheduledPods's namespace
   409â†’		otherPods := 0              // Count of pods in other namespaces
   410â†’		for _, pod := range pods {
   411â†’			if pod.Namespace == scheduledPod.Namespace {
   412â†’				localPodNames = append(localPodNames, pod.Name)
   413â†’			} else {
   414â†’				otherPods++
   415â†’			}
   416â†’		}
   417â†’
   418â†’		var msg string
   419â†’		if len(localPodNames) > 0 {
   420â†’			msg = fmt.Sprintf("Volume is already used by pod(s) %s", strings.Join(localPodNames, ", "))
   421â†’			if otherPods > 0 {
   422â†’				msg = fmt.Sprintf("%s and %d pod(s) in different namespaces", msg, otherPods)
   423â†’			}
   424â†’		} else {
   425â†’			// No local pods, there are pods only in different namespaces.
   426â†’			msg = fmt.Sprintf("Volume is already used by %d pod(s) in different namespaces", otherPods)
   427â†’		}
   428â†’		simpleMsg, _ := volumeToAttach.GenerateMsg("Multi-Attach error", msg)
   429â†’		rc.recorder.Eventf(scheduledPod, v1.EventTypeWarning, kevents.FailedAttachVolume, simpleMsg)
   430â†’	}
   431â†’
   432â†’	// Log all pods for system admin
   433â†’	logger.Info("Multi-Attach error: volume is already used by pods", "pods", klog.KObjSlice(pods), "attachedTo", otherNodesStr, "volume", volumeToAttach)
   434â†’}

This reconciles state for the attach/detach controller. That\n// reconciles state for the kubelet volume manager.\ntype Reconciler interface {\n\t// Starts running the reconciliation loop which executes periodically, checks\n\t// if volumes that should be attached are attached and volumes that should\n\t// be detached are detached. If not, it will trigger attach/detach\n\t// operations to rectify.\n\tRun(ctx context.Context)\n}\n\n// NewReconciler returns a new instance of Reconciler that waits loopPeriod\n// between successive executions.\n// loopPeriod is the amount of time the reconciler loop waits between\n// successive executions.\n// maxWaitForUnmountDuration is the max amount of time the reconciler will wait\n// for the volume to be safely unmounted, after this it will detach the volume\n// anyway (to handle crashed/unavailable nodes). If during this time the volume\n// becomes used by a new pod, the detach request will be aborted and the timer\n// cleared.\nfunc NewReconciler(\n\tloopPeriod time.Duration,\n\tmaxWaitForUnmountDuration time.Duration,\n\tsyncDuration time.Duration,\n\tdisableReconciliationSync bool,\n\tdisableForceDetachOnTimeout bool,\n\tdesiredStateOfWorld cache.DesiredStateOfWorld,\n\tactualStateOfWorld cache.ActualStateOfWorld,\n\tattacherDetacher operationexecutor.OperationExecutor,\n\tnodeStatusUpdater statusupdater.NodeStatusUpdater,\n\tnodeLister corelisters.NodeLister,\n\trecorder record.EventRecorder) Reconciler {\n\treturn &reconciler{\n\t\tloopPeriod:                  loopPeriod,\n\t\tmaxWaitForUnmountDuration:   maxWaitForUnmountDuration,\n\t\tsyncDuration:                syncDuration,\n\t\tdisableReconciliationSync:   disableReconciliationSync,\n\t\tdisableForceDetachOnTimeout: disableForceDetachOnTimeout,\n\t\tdesiredStateOfWorld:         desiredStateOfWorld,\n\t\tactualStateOfWorld:          actualStateOfWorld,\n\t\tattacherDetacher:            attacherDetacher,\n\t\tnodeStatusUpdater:           nodeStatusUpdater,\n\t\tnodeLister:                  nodeLister,\n\t\ttimeOfLastSync:              time.Now(),\n\t\trecorder:                    recorder,\n\t}\n}\n\ntype reconciler struct {\n\tloopPeriod                  time.Duration\n\tmaxWaitForUnmountDuration   time.Duration\n\tsyncDuration                time.Duration\n\tdesiredStateOfWorld         cache.DesiredStateOfWorld\n\tactualStateOfWorld          cache.ActualStateOfWorld\n\tattacherDetacher            operationexecutor.OperationExecutor\n\tnodeStatusUpdater           statusupdater.NodeStatusUpdater\n\tnodeLister                  corelisters.NodeLister\n\ttimeOfLastSync              time.Time\n\tdisableReconciliationSync   bool\n\tdisableForceDetachOnTimeout bool\n\trecorder                    record.EventRecorder\n}\n\nfunc (rc *reconciler) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n\twait.UntilWithContext(ctx, rc.reconciliationLoopFunc(ctx), rc.loopPeriod)\n}\n\n// reconciliationLoopFunc this can be disabled via cli option disableReconciliation.\n// It periodically checks whether the attached volumes from actual state\n// are still attached to the node and update the status if they are not.\nfunc (rc *reconciler) reconciliationLoopFunc(ctx context.Context) func(context.Context) {\n\treturn func(ctx context.Context) {\n\n\t\trc.reconcile(ctx)\n\t\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\t\tif rc.disableReconciliationSync {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Skipping reconciling attached volumes still attached since it is disabled via the command line\")\n\t\t} else if rc.syncDuration < time.Second {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Skipping reconciling attached volumes still attached since it is set to less than one second via the command line\")\n\t\t} else if time.Since(rc.timeOfLastSync) > rc.syncDuration {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Starting reconciling attached volumes still attached\")\n\t\t\trc.sync()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (rc *reconciler) sync() {\n\tdefer rc.updateSyncTime()\n\trc.syncStates()\n}\n\nfunc (rc *reconciler) updateSyncTime() {\n\trc.timeOfLastSync = time.Now()\n}\n\nfunc (rc *reconciler) syncStates() {\n\tvolumesPerNode := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachedVolumesPerNode()\n\trc.attacherDetacher.VerifyVolumesAreAttached(volumesPerNode, rc.actualStateOfWorld)\n}\n\n// hasOutOfServiceTaint returns true if the node has out-of-service taint present.\nfunc (rc *reconciler) hasOutOfServiceTaint(nodeName types.NodeName) (bool, error) {\n\tnode, err := rc.nodeLister.Get(string(nodeName))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\treturn taints.TaintKeyExists(node.Spec.Taints, v1.TaintNodeOutOfService), nil\n}\n\n// nodeIsHealthy returns true if the node looks healthy.\nfunc (rc *reconciler) nodeIsHealthy(nodeName types.NodeName) (bool, error) {\n\tnode, err := rc.nodeLister.Get(string(nodeName))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\treturn nodeutil.IsNodeReady(node), nil\n}\n\nfunc (rc *reconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context) {\n\t// Detaches are triggered before attaches so that volumes referenced by\n\t// pods that are rescheduled to a different node are detached first.\n\n\t// Ensure volumes that should be detached are detached.\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tfor _, attachedVolume := range rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachedVolumes() {\n\t\tif !rc.desiredStateOfWorld.VolumeExists(\n\t\t\tattachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName) {\n\n\t\t\t// Check whether there already exist an operation pending, and don't even\n\t\t\t// try to start an operation if there is already one running.\n\t\t\t// This check must be done before we do any other checks, as otherwise the other checks\n\t\t\t// may pass while at the same time the volume leaves the pending state, resulting in\n\t\t\t// double detach attempts\n\t\t\t// The operation key format is different depending on whether the volume\n\t\t\t// allows multi attach across different nodes.\n\t\t\tif util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(attachedVolume.VolumeSpec) {\n\t\t\t\tif !rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationSafeToRetry(attachedVolume.VolumeName, \"\" /* podName */, attachedVolume.NodeName, operationexecutor.DetachOperationName) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlogger.V(10).Info(\"Operation for volume is already running or still in exponential backoff for node. Can't start detach\", \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), \"volumeName\", attachedVolume.VolumeName)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif !rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationSafeToRetry(attachedVolume.VolumeName, \"\" /* podName */, \"\" /* nodeName */, operationexecutor.DetachOperationName) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlogger.V(10).Info(\"Operation for volume is already running or still in exponential backoff in the cluster. Can't start detach for node\", \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), \"volumeName\", attachedVolume.VolumeName)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Because the detach operation updates the ActualStateOfWorld before\n\t\t\t// marking itself complete, it's possible for the volume to be removed\n\t\t\t// from the ActualStateOfWorld between the GetAttachedVolumes() check\n\t\t\t// and the IsOperationSafeToRetry() check above.\n\t\t\t// Check the ActualStateOfWorld again to avoid issuing an unnecessary\n\t\t\t// detach.\n\t\t\t// See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/93902\n\t\t\tattachState := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachState(attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)\n\t\t\tif attachState == cache.AttachStateDetached {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Volume detached--skipping\", \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), \"volumeName\", attachedVolume.VolumeName)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Set the detach request time\n\t\t\telapsedTime, err := rc.actualStateOfWorld.SetDetachRequestTime(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Cannot trigger detach because it fails to set detach request time with error\")\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check whether the umount drain timer expired\n\t\t\tmaxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired := elapsedTime > rc.maxWaitForUnmountDuration\n\n\t\t\tisHealthy, err := rc.nodeIsHealthy(attachedVolume.NodeName)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to get health of node\", \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Force detach volumes from unhealthy nodes after maxWaitForUnmountDuration if force detach is enabled\n\t\t\t// Ensure that the timeout condition checks this correctly so that the correct metric is updated below\n\t\t\tforceDetatchTimeoutExpired := maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && !rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout\n\t\t\tif maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Drain timeout expired for volume but disableForceDetachOnTimeout was set\", \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), \"volumeName\", attachedVolume.VolumeName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tforceDetach := !isHealthy && forceDetatchTimeoutExpired\n\n\t\t\thasOutOfServiceTaint, err := rc.hasOutOfServiceTaint(attachedVolume.NodeName)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to get taint specs for node\", \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\",    205â†’			}
   206â†’
   207â†’			// Set the detach request time
   208â†’			elapsedTime, err := rc.actualStateOfWorld.SetDetachRequestTime(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   209â†’			if err != nil {
   210â†’				logger.Error(err, "Cannot trigger detach because it fails to set detach request time with error")
   211â†’				continue
   212â†’			}
   213â†’			// Check whether the umount drain timer expired
   214â†’			maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired := elapsedTime > rc.maxWaitForUnmountDuration
   215â†’
   216â†’			isHealthy, err := rc.nodeIsHealthy(attachedVolume.NodeName)
   217â†’			if err != nil {
   218â†’				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get health of node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   219â†’			}
   220â†’
   221â†’			// Force detach volumes from unhealthy nodes after maxWaitForUnmountDuration if force detach is enabled
   222â†’			// Ensure that the timeout condition checks this correctly so that the correct metric is updated below
   223â†’			forceDetatchTimeoutExpired := maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && !rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout
   224â†’			if maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout {
   225â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Drain timeout expired for volume but disableForceDetachOnTimeout was set", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   226â†’			}
   227â†’			forceDetach := !isHealthy && forceDetatchTimeoutExpired
   228â†’
   229â†’			hasOutOfServiceTaint, err := rc.hasOutOfServiceTaint(attachedVolume.NodeName)
   230â†’			if err != nil {
   231â†’				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get taint specs for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   232â†’			}
   233â†’
   234â†’			// Check whether volume is still mounted. Skip detach if it is still mounted unless we have
   235â†’			// decided to force detach or the node has `node.kubernetes.io/out-of-service` taint.
   236â†’			if attachedVolume.MountedByNode && !forceDetach && !hasOutOfServiceTaint {
   237â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Cannot detach volume because it is still mounted", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   238â†’				continue
   239â†’			}
   240â†’
   241â†’			// Before triggering volume detach, mark volume as detached and update the node status
   242â†’			// If it fails to update node status, skip detach volume
   243â†’			// If volume detach operation fails, the volume needs to be added back to report as attached so that node status
   244â†’			// has the correct volume attachment information.
   245â†’			err = rc.actualStateOfWorld.RemoveVolumeFromReportAsAttached(attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   246â†’			if err != nil {
   247â†’				logger.V(5).Info("RemoveVolumeFromReportAsAttached failed while removing volume from node",
   248â†’					"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),
   249â†’					"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName,
   250â†’					"err", err)
   251â†’			}
   252â†’
   253â†’			// Update Node Status to indicate volume is no longer safe to mount.
   254â†’			err = rc.nodeStatusUpdater.UpdateNodeStatusForNode(logger, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   255â†’			if err != nil {
   256â†’				// Skip detaching this volume if unable to update node status
   257â†’				logger.Error(err, "UpdateNodeStatusForNode failed while attempting to report volume as attached", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   258â†’				// Add volume back to ReportAsAttached if UpdateNodeStatusForNode call failed so that node status updater will add it back to VolumeAttached list.
   259â†’				// It is needed here too because DetachVolume is not call actually and we keep the data consistency for every reconcile.
   260â†’				rc.actualStateOfWorld.AddVolumeToReportAsAttached(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   261â†’				continue
   262â†’			}
   263â†’
   264â†’			// Trigger detach volume which requires verifying safe to detach step
   265â†’			// If forceDetatchTimeoutExpired is true, skip verifySafeToDetach check
   266â†’			// If the node has node.kubernetes.io/out-of-service taint with NoExecute effect, skip verifySafeToDetach check
   267â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Starting attacherDetacher.DetachVolume", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   268â†’			if hasOutOfServiceTaint {
   269â†’				logger.V(4).Info("node has out-of-service taint", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   270â†’			}
   271â†’			verifySafeToDetach := !(forceDetatchTimeoutExpired || hasOutOfServiceTaint)
   272â†’			err = rc.attacherDetacher.DetachVolume(logger, attachedVolume.AttachedVolume, verifySafeToDetach, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   273â†’			if err == nil {
   274â†’				if verifySafeToDetach { // normal detach
   275â†’					logger.Info("attacherDetacher.DetachVolume started", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   276â†’				} else { // force detach
   277â†’					if forceDetatchTimeoutExpired {
   278â†’						metrics.RecordForcedDetachMetric(metrics.ForceDetachReasonTimeout)
   279â†’						logger.Info("attacherDetacher.DetachVolume started: this volume is not safe to detach, but maxWaitForUnmountDuration expired, force detaching",
   280â†’							"duration", rc.maxWaitForUnmountDuration,
   281â†’							"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),
   282â†’							"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   283â†’					} else {
   284â†’						metrics.RecordForcedDetachMetric(metrics.ForceDetachReasonOutOfService)
   285â†’						logger.Info("attacherDetacher.DetachVolume started: node has out-of-service taint, force detaching",
   286â†’							"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),
   287â†’							"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   288â†’					}
   289â†’				}
   290â†’			}
   291â†’			if err != nil {
   292â†’				// Add volume back to ReportAsAttached if DetachVolume call failed so that node status updater will add it back to VolumeAttached list.
   293â†’				// This function is also called during executing the volume detach operation in operation_generoator.
   294â†’				// It is needed here too because DetachVolume call might fail before executing the actual operation in operation_executor (e.g., cannot find volume plugin etc.)
   295â†’				rc.actualStateOfWorld.AddVolumeToReportAsAttached(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   296â†’
   297â†’				if !exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff(err) {
   298â†’					// Ignore exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff errors, they are expected.
   299â†’					// Log all other errors.
   300â†’					logger.Error(err, "attacherDetacher.DetachVolume failed to start", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   301â†’				}
   302â†’			}
   303â†’		}
   304â†’	}
   305â†’
   306â†’	rc.attachDesiredVolumes(logger)
   307â†’
   308â†’	// Update Node Status
   309â†’	err := rc.nodeStatusUpdater.UpdateNodeStatuses(logger)
   310â†’	if err != nil {
   311â†’		logger.Info("UpdateNodeStatuses failed", "err", err)
   312â†’	}
   313â†’}
   314â†’
   315â†’func (rc *reconciler) attachDesiredVolumes(logger klog.Logger) {
   316â†’	// Ensure volumes that should be attached are attached.
   317â†’	for _, volumeToAttach := range rc.desiredStateOfWorld.GetVolumesToAttach() {
   318â†’		if util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(volumeToAttach.VolumeSpec) {
   319â†’			// Don't even try to start an operation if there is already one running for the given volume and node.
   320â†’			if rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationPending(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, volumeToAttach.NodeName) {
   321â†’				logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running for node. Can't start attach", "node", klog.KRef("", string(volumeToAttach.NodeName)), "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   322â†’				continue
   323â†’			}
   324â†’		} else {
   325â†’			// Don't even try to start an operation if there is already one running for the given volume
   326â†’			if rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationPending(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, "" /* nodeName */) {
   327â†’				logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running. Can't start attach for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(volumeToAttach.NodeName)), "volumeNames", volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   328â†’				continue
   329â†’			}
   330â†’		}
   331â†’
   332â†’		// Because the attach operation updates the ActualStateOfWorld before
   333â†’		// marking itself complete, IsOperationPending() must be checked before
   334â†’		// GetAttachState() to guarantee the ActualStateOfWorld is
   335â†’		// up-to-date when it's read.
   336â†’		// See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/93902
   337â†’		attachState := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachState(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   338â†’		if attachState == cache.AttachStateAttached {
   339â†’			// Volume/Node exists, touch it to reset detachRequestedTime
   340â†’			logger.V(10).Info("Volume attached--touching", "volume", volumeToAttach)
   341â†’			rc.actualStateOfWorld.ResetDetachRequestTime(logger, volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   342â†’			continue
   343â†’		}
   344â†’
   345â†’		if !util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(volumeToAttach.VolumeSpec) {
   346â†’			nodes := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetNodesForAttachedVolume(volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   347â†’			if len(nodes) > 0 {
   348â†’				if !volumeToAttach.MultiAttachErrorReported {
   349â†’					rc.reportMultiAttachError(logger, volumeToAttach, nodes)
   350â†’					rc.desiredStateOfWorld.SetMultiAttachError(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   351â†’				}
   352â†’				continue
   353â†’			}
   354â†’		}
   355â†’
   356â†’		// Volume/Node doesn't exist, spawn a goroutine to attach it
   357â†’		logger.V(5).Info("Starting attacherDetacher.AttachVolume", "volume", volumeToAttach)
   358â†’		err := rc.attacherDetacher.AttachVolume(logger, volumeToAttach.VolumeToAttach, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   359â†’		if err == nil {
   360â†’			logger.Info("attacherDetacher.AttachVolume started", "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "nodeName", volumeToAttach.NodeName, "scheduledPods", klog.KObjSlice(volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods))
   361â†’		}
   362â†’		if err != nil && !exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff(err) {
   363â†’			// Ignore exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff errors, they are expected.
   364â†’			// Log all other errors.
   365â†’			logger.Error(err, "attacherDetacher.AttachVolume failed to start", "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "nodeName", volumeToAttach.NodeName, "scheduledPods", klog.KObjSlice(volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods))
   366â†’		}
   367â†’	}
   368â†’}
   369â†’
   370â†’// reportMultiAttachError sends events and logs situation that a volume that
   371â†’// should be attached to a node is already attached to different node(s).
   372â†’func (rc *reconciler) reportMultiAttachError(logger klog.Logger, volumeToAttach cache.VolumeToAttach, nodes []types.NodeName) {
   373â†’	// Filter out the current node from list of nodes where the volume is
   374â†’	// attached.
   375â†’	// Some methods need []string, some other needs []NodeName, collect both.
   376â†’	// In theory, these arrays should have always only one element - the
   377â†’	// controller does not allow more than one attachment. But use array just
   378â†’	// in case...
   379â†’	otherNodes := []types.NodeName{}
   380â†’	otherNodesStr := []string{}
   381â†’	for _, node := range nodes {
   382â†’		if node != volumeToAttach.NodeName {
   383â†’			otherNodes = append(otherNodes, node)
   384â†’			otherNodesStr = append(otherNodesStr, string(node))
   385â†’		}
   386â†’	}
   387â†’
   388â†’	// Get list of pods that use the volume on the other nodes.
   389â†’	pods := rc.desiredStateOfWorld.GetVolumePodsOnNodes(otherNodes, volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   390â†’	if len(pods) == 0 {
   391â†’		// We did not find any pods that requests the volume. The pod must have been deleted already.
   392â†’		simpleMsg, _ := volumeToAttach.GenerateMsg("Multi-Attach error", "Volume is already exclusively attached to one node and can't be attached to another")
   393â†’		for _, pod := range volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods {
   394â†’			rc.recorder.Eventf(pod, v1.EventTypeWarning, kevents.FailedAttachVolume, simpleMsg)
   395â†’		}
   396â†’		// Log detailed message to system admin
   397â†’		logger.Info("Multi-Attach error: volume is already exclusively attached and can't be attached to another node", "attachedTo", otherNodesStr, "volume", volumeToAttach)
   398â†’		return
   399â†’	}
   400â†’
   401â†’	// There are pods that require the volume and run on another node. Typically
   402â†’	// it's user error, e.g. a ReplicaSet uses a PVC and has >1 replicas. Let
   403â†’	// the user know what pods are blocking the volume.
   404â†’	for _, scheduledPod := range volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods {
   405â†’		// Each scheduledPod must get a custom message. They can run in
   406â†’		// different namespaces and user of a namespace should not see names of
   407â†’		// pods in other namespaces.
   408â†’		localPodNames := []string{} // Names of pods in scheduledPods's namespace
   409â†’		otherPods := 0              // Count of pods in other namespaces
   410â†’		for _, pod := range pods {
   411â†’			if pod.Namespace == scheduledPod.Namespace {
   412â†’				localPodNames = append(localPodNames, pod.Name)
   413â†’			} else {
   414â†’				otherPods++
   415â†’			}
   416â†’		}
   417â†’
   418â†’		var msg string
   419â†’		if len(localPodNames) > 0 {
   420â†’			msg = fmt.Sprintf("Volume is already used by pod(s) %s", strings.Join(localPodNames, ", "))
   421â†’			if otherPods > 0 {
   422â†’				msg = fmt.Sprintf("%s and %d pod(s) in different namespaces", msg, otherPods)
   423â†’			}
   424â†’		} else {
   425â†’			// No local pods, there are pods only in different namespaces.
   426â†’			msg = fmt.Sprintf("Volume is already used by %d pod(s) in different namespaces", otherPods)
   427â†’		}
   428â†’		simpleMsg, _ := volumeToAttach.GenerateMsg("Multi-Attach error", msg)
   429â†’		rc.recorder.Eventf(scheduledPod, v1.EventTypeWarning, kevents.FailedAttachVolume, simpleMsg)
   430â†’	}
   431â†’
   432â†’	// Log all pods for system admin
   433â†’	logger.Info("Multi-Attach error: volume is already used by pods", "pods", klog.KObjSlice(pods), "attachedTo", otherNodesStr, "volume", volumeToAttach)
   434â†’}

    16â†’
    17â†’// Package reconciler implements interfaces that attempt to reconcile the
    18â†’// desired state of the with the actual state of the world by triggering
    19â†’// actions.
    20â†’package reconciler
    21â†’
    22â†’import (
    23â†’	"context"
    24â†’	"fmt"
    25â†’	"strings"
    26â†’	"time"
    27â†’
    28â†’	v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    29â†’	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
    30â†’	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    31â†’	corelisters "k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1"
    32â†’	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
    33â†’	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    34â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/volume/attachdetach/cache"
    35â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/volume/attachdetach/metrics"
    36â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/volume/attachdetach/statusupdater"
    37â†’	kevents "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/events"
    38â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/goroutinemap/exponentialbackoff"
    39â†’	nodeutil "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/node"
    40â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/taints"
    41â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume/util"
    42â†’	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume/util/operationexecutor"
    43â†’)
    44â†’
    45â†’// Reconciler runs a periodic loop to reconcile the desired state of the world with
    46â†’// the actual state of the world by triggering attach detach operations.
    47â†’// Note: This is distinct from the Reconciler implemented by the kubelet volume
    48â†’// manager. This reconciles state for the attach/detach controller. That
    49â†’// reconciles state for the kubelet volume manager.
    50â†’type Reconciler interface {
    51â†’	// Starts running the reconciliation loop which executes periodically, checks
    52â†’	// if volumes that should be attached are attached and volumes that should
    53â†’	// be detached are detached. If not, it will trigger attach/detach
    54â†’	// operations to rectify.
    55â†’	Run(ctx context.Context)
    56â†’}
    57â†’
    58â†’// NewReconciler returns a new instance of Reconciler that waits loopPeriod
    59â†’// between successive executions.
    60â†’// loopPeriod is the amount of time the reconciler loop waits between
    61â†’// successive executions.
    62â†’// maxWaitForUnmountDuration is the max amount of time the reconciler will wait
    63â†’// for the volume to be safely unmounted, after this it will detach the volume
    64â†’// anyway (to handle crashed/unavailable nodes). If during this time the volume
    65â†’// becomes used by a new pod, the detach request will be aborted and the timer
    66â†’// cleared.
    67â†’func NewReconciler(
    68â†’	loopPeriod time.Duration,
    69â†’	maxWaitForUnmountDuration time.Duration,
    70â†’	syncDuration time.Duration,
    71â†’	disableReconciliationSync bool,
    72â†’	disableForceDetachOnTimeout bool,
    73â†’	desiredStateOfWorld cache.DesiredStateOfWorld,
    74â†’	actualStateOfWorld cache.ActualStateOfWorld,
    75â†’	attacherDetacher operationexecutor.OperationExecutor,
    76â†’	nodeStatusUpdater statusupdater.NodeStatusUpdater,
    77â†’	nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister,
    78â†’	recorder record.EventRecorder) Reconciler {
    79â†’	return &reconciler{
    80â†’		loopPeriod:                  loopPeriod,
    81â†’		maxWaitForUnmountDuration:   maxWaitForUnmountDuration,
    82â†’		syncDuration:                syncDuration,
    83â†’		disableReconciliationSync:   disableReconciliationSync,
    84â†’		disableForceDetachOnTimeout: disableForceDetachOnTimeout,
    85â†’		desiredStateOfWorld:         desiredStateOfWorld,
    86â†’		actualStateOfWorld:          actualStateOfWorld,
    87â†’		attacherDetacher:            attacherDetacher,
    88â†’		nodeStatusUpdater:           nodeStatusUpdater,
    89â†’		nodeLister:                  nodeLister,
    90â†’		timeOfLastSync:              time.Now(),
    91â†’		recorder:                    recorder,
    92â†’	}
    93â†’}
    94â†’
    95â†’type reconciler struct {
    96â†’	loopPeriod                  time.Duration
    97â†’	maxWaitForUnmountDuration   time.Duration
    98â†’	syncDuration                time.Duration
    99â†’	desiredStateOfWorld         cache.DesiredStateOfWorld
   100â†’	actualStateOfWorld          cache.ActualStateOfWorld
   101â†’	attacherDetacher            operationexecutor.OperationExecutor
   102â†’	nodeStatusUpdater           statusupdater.NodeStatusUpdater
   103â†’	nodeLister                  corelisters.NodeLister
   104â†’	timeOfLastSync              time.Time
   105â†’	disableReconciliationSync   bool
   106â†’	disableForceDetachOnTimeout bool
   107â†’	recorder                    record.EventRecorder
   108â†’}
   109â†’
   110â†’func (rc *reconciler) Run(ctx context.Context) {
   111â†’	wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, rc.reconciliationLoopFunc(ctx), rc.loopPeriod)
   112â†’}
   113â†’
   114â†’// reconciliationLoopFunc this can be disabled via cli option disableReconciliation.
   115â†’// It periodically checks whether the attached volumes from actual state
   116â†’// are still attached to the node and update the status if they are not.
   117â†’func (rc *reconciler) reconciliationLoopFunc(ctx context.Context) func(context.Context) {
   118â†’	return func(ctx context.Context) {
   119â†’
   120â†’		rc.reconcile(ctx)
   121â†’		logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
   122â†’		if rc.disableReconciliationSync {
   123â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Skipping reconciling attached volumes still attached since it is disabled via the command line")
   124â†’		} else if rc.syncDuration < time.Second {
   125â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Skipping reconciling attached volumes still attached since it is set to less than one second via the command line")
   126â†’		} else if time.Since(rc.timeOfLastSync) > rc.syncDuration {
   127â†’			logger.V(5).Info("Starting reconciling attached volumes still attached")
   128â†’			rc.sync()
   129â†’		}
   130â†’	}
   131â†’}
   132â†’
   133â†’func (rc *reconciler) sync() {
   134â†’	defer rc.updateSyncTime()
   135â†’	rc.syncStates()
   136â†’}
   137â†’
   138â†’func (rc *reconciler) updateSyncTime() {
   139â†’	rc.timeOfLastSync = time.Now()
   140â†’}
   141â†’
   142â†’func (rc *reconciler) syncStates() {
   143â†’	volumesPerNode := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachedVolumesPerNode()
   144â†’	rc.attacherDetacher.VerifyVolumesAreAttached(volumesPerNode, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   145â†’}
   146â†’
   147â†’// hasOutOfServiceTaint returns true if the node has out-of-service taint present.
   148â†’func (rc *reconciler) hasOutOfServiceTaint(nodeName types.NodeName) (bool, error) {
   149â†’	node, err := rc.nodeLister.Get(string(nodeName))
   150â†’	if err != nil {
   151â†’		return false, err
   152â†’	}
   153â†’	return taints.TaintKeyExists(node.Spec.Taints, v1.TaintNodeOutOfService), nil
   154â†’}
   155â†’
   156â†’// nodeIsHealthy returns true if the node looks healthy.
   157â†’func (rc *reconciler) nodeIsHealthy(nodeName types.NodeName) (bool, error) {
   158â†’	node, err := rc.nodeLister.Get(string(nodeName))
   159â†’	if err != nil {
   160â†’		return false, err
   161â†’	}
   162â†’	return nodeutil.IsNodeReady(node), nil
   163â†’}
   164â†’
   165â†’func (rc *reconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context) {

   167â†’	// pods that are rescheduled to a different node are detached first.

   184â†’					logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running or still in exponential backoff for node. Can't start detach", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   185â†’					continue
   186â†’				}
   187â†’			} else {
   188â†’				if !rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationSafeToRetry(attachedVolume.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, "" /* nodeName */, operationexecutor.DetachOperationName) {
   189â†’					logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running or still in exponential backoff in the cluster. Can't start detach for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   190â†’					continue
   191â†’				}
   192â†’			}

   195â†’			// marking itself complete, it's possible for the volume to be removed
   196â†’			// from the ActualStateOfWorld between the GetAttachedVolumes() check

   199â†’			// detach.

   202â†’			if attachState == cache.AttachStateDetached {
   203â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Volume detached--skipping", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   204â†’				continue
   205â†’			}
   206â†’
   207â†’			// Set the detach request time
   208â†’			elapsedTime, err := rc.actualStateOfWorld.SetDetachRequestTime(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   209â†’			if err != nil {

   214â†’			maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired := elapsedTime > rc.maxWaitForUnmountDuration
   215â†’
   216â†’			isHealthy, err := rc.nodeIsHealthy(attachedVolume.NodeName)
   217â†’			if err != nil {
   218â†’				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get health of node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   219â†’			}
   220â†’
   221â†’			// Force detach volumes from unhealthy nodes after maxWaitForUnmountDuration if force detach is enabled
   222â†’			// Ensure that the timeout condition checks this correctly so that the correct metric is updated below
   223â†’			forceDetatchTimeoutExpired := maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && !rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout
   224â†’			if maxWaitForUnmountDurationExpired && rc.disableForceDetachOnTimeout {
   225â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Drain timeout expired for volume but disableForceDetachOnTimeout was set", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   226â†’			}
   227â†’			forceDetach := !isHealthy && forceDetatchTimeoutExpired
   228â†’
   229â†’			hasOutOfServiceTaint, err := rc.hasOutOfServiceTaint(attachedVolume.NodeName)
   230â†’			if err != nil {
   231â†’				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get taint specs for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   232â†’			}
   233â†’
   234â†’			// Check whether volume is still mounted. Skip detach if it is still mounted unless we have
   235â†’			// decided to force detach or the node has `node.kubernetes.io/out-of-service` taint.
   236â†’			if attachedVolume.MountedByNode && !forceDetach && !hasOutOfServiceTaint {
   237â†’				logger.V(5).Info("Cannot detach volume because it is still mounted", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   238â†’				continue
   239â†’			}
   240â†’
   241â†’			// Before triggering volume detach, mark volume as detached and update the node status
   242â†’			// If it fails to update node status, skip detach volume
   243â†’			// If volume detach operation fails, the volume needs to be added back to report as attached so that node status
   244â†’			// has the correct volume attachment information.
   245â†’			err = rc.actualStateOfWorld.RemoveVolumeFromReportAsAttached(attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   246â†’			if err != nil {
   247â†’				logger.V(5).Info("RemoveVolumeFromReportAsAttached failed while removing volume from node",
   248â†’					"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),
   249â†’					"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName,
   250â†’					"err", err)
   251â†’			}
   252â†’
   253â†’			// Update Node Status to indicate volume is no longer safe to mount.
   254â†’			err = rc.nodeStatusUpdater.UpdateNodeStatusForNode(logger, attachedVolume.NodeName)

   259â†’				// It is needed here too because DetachVolume is not call actually and we keep the data consistency for every reconcile.

   265â†’			// If forceDetatchTimeoutExpired is true, skip verifySafeToDetach check
   266â†’			// If the node has node.kubernetes.io/out-of-service taint with NoExecute effect, skip verifySafeToDetach check

   268â†’			if hasOutOfServiceTaint {
   269â†’				logger.V(4).Info("node has out-of-service taint", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)))
   270â†’			}
   271â†’			verifySafeToDetach := !(forceDetatchTimeoutExpired || hasOutOfServiceTaint)
   272â†’			err = rc.attacherDetacher.DetachVolume(logger, attachedVolume.AttachedVolume, verifySafeToDetach, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   273â†’			if err == nil {
   274â†’				if verifySafeToDetach { // normal detach
   275â†’					logger.Info("attacherDetacher.DetachVolume started", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   276â†’				} else { // force detach
   277â†’					if forceDetatchTimeoutExpired {
   278â†’						metrics.RecordForcedDetachMetric(metrics.ForceDetachReasonTimeout)
   279â†’						logger.Info("attacherDetacher.DetachVolume started: this volume is not safe to detach, but maxWaitForUnmountDuration expired, force detaching",
   280â†’							"duration", rc.maxWaitForUnmountDuration,
   281â†’							"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),
   282â†’							"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   283â†’					} else {
   284â†’						metrics.RecordForcedDetachMetric(metrics.ForceDetachReasonOutOfService)
   285â†’						logger.Info("attacherDetacher.DetachVolume started: node has out-of-service taint, force detaching",
   286â†’							"node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)),
   287â†’							"volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   288â†’					}
   289â†’				}
   290â†’			}
   291â†’			if err != nil {
   292â†’				// Add volume back to ReportAsAttached if DetachVolume call failed so that node status updater will add it back to VolumeAttached list.
   293â†’				// This function is also called during executing the volume detach operation in operation_generoator.
   294â†’				// It is needed here too because DetachVolume call might fail before executing the actual operation in operation_executor (e.g., cannot find volume plugin etc.)
   295â†’				rc.actualStateOfWorld.AddVolumeToReportAsAttached(logger, attachedVolume.VolumeName, attachedVolume.NodeName)
   296â†’
   297â†’				if !exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff(err) {
   298â†’					// Ignore exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff errors, they are expected.
   299â†’					// Log all other errors.
   300â†’					logger.Error(err, "attacherDetacher.DetachVolume failed to start", "node", klog.KRef("", string(attachedVolume.NodeName)), "volumeName", attachedVolume.VolumeName)
   301â†’				}
   302â†’			}
   303â†’		}
   304â†’	}
   305â†’
   306â†’	rc.attachDesiredVolumes(logger)
   307â†’
   308â†’	// Update Node Status
   309â†’	err := rc.nodeStatusUpdater.UpdateNodeStatuses(logger)
   310â†’	if err != nil {
   311â†’		logger.Info("UpdateNodeStatuses failed", "err", err)
   312â†’	}
   313â†’}
   314â†’
   315â†’func (rc *reconciler) attachDesiredVolumes(logger klog.Logger) {
   316â†’	// Ensure volumes that should be attached are attached.
   317â†’	for _, volumeToAttach := range rc.desiredStateOfWorld.GetVolumesToAttach() {
   318â†’		if util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(volumeToAttach.VolumeSpec) {
   319â†’			// Don't even try to start an operation if there is already one running for the given volume and node.
   320â†’			if rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationPending(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, volumeToAttach.NodeName) {
   321â†’				logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running for node. Can't start attach", "node", klog.KRef("", string(volumeToAttach.NodeName)), "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   322â†’				continue
   323â†’			}
   324â†’		} else {
   325â†’			// Don't even try to start an operation if there is already one running for the given volume
   326â†’			if rc.attacherDetacher.IsOperationPending(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "" /* podName */, "" /* nodeName */) {
   327â†’				logger.V(10).Info("Operation for volume is already running. Can't start attach for node", "node", klog.KRef("", string(volumeToAttach.NodeName)), "volumeNames", volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   328â†’				continue
   329â†’			}
   330â†’		}
   331â†’
   332â†’		// Because the attach operation updates the ActualStateOfWorld before
   333â†’		// marking itself complete, IsOperationPending() must be checked before
   334â†’		// GetAttachState() to guarantee the ActualStateOfWorld is
   335â†’		// up-to-date when it's read.
   336â†’		// See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/93902
   337â†’		attachState := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetAttachState(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   338â†’		if attachState == cache.AttachStateAttached {
   339â†’			// Volume/Node exists, touch it to reset detachRequestedTime
   340â†’			logger.V(10).Info("Volume attached--touching", "volume", volumeToAttach)
   341â†’			rc.actualStateOfWorld.ResetDetachRequestTime(logger, volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   342â†’			continue
   343â†’		}
   344â†’
   345â†’		if !util.IsMultiAttachAllowed(volumeToAttach.VolumeSpec) {
   346â†’			nodes := rc.actualStateOfWorld.GetNodesForAttachedVolume(volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   347â†’			if len(nodes) > 0 {
   348â†’				if !volumeToAttach.MultiAttachErrorReported {
   349â†’					rc.reportMultiAttachError(logger, volumeToAttach, nodes)
   350â†’					rc.desiredStateOfWorld.SetMultiAttachError(volumeToAttach.VolumeName, volumeToAttach.NodeName)
   351â†’				}
   352â†’				continue
   353â†’			}
   354â†’		}
   355â†’
   356â†’		// Volume/Node doesn't exist, spawn a goroutine to attach it
   357â†’		logger.V(5).Info("Starting attacherDetacher.AttachVolume", "volume", volumeToAttach)
   358â†’		err := rc.attacherDetacher.AttachVolume(logger, volumeToAttach.VolumeToAttach, rc.actualStateOfWorld)
   359â†’		if err == nil {
   360â†’			logger.Info("attacherDetacher.AttachVolume started", "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "nodeName", volumeToAttach.NodeName, "scheduledPods", klog.KObjSlice(volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods))
   361â†’		}
   362â†’		if err != nil && !exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff(err) {
   363â†’			// Ignore exponentialbackoff.IsExponentialBackoff errors, they are expected.
   364â†’			// Log all other errors.
   365â†’			logger.Error(err, "attacherDetacher.AttachVolume failed to start", "volumeName", volumeToAttach.VolumeName, "nodeName", volumeToAttach.NodeName, "scheduledPods", klog.KObjSlice(volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods))
   366â†’		}
   367â†’	}
   368â†’}
   369â†’
   370â†’// reportMultiAttachError sends events and logs situation that a volume that
   371â†’// should be attached to a node is already attached to different node(s).
   372â†’func (rc *reconciler) reportMultiAttachError(logger klog.Logger, volumeToAttach cache.VolumeToAttach, nodes []types.NodeName) {
   373â†’	// Filter out the current node from list of nodes where the volume is
   374â†’	// attached.
   375â†’	// Some methods need []string, some other needs []NodeName, collect both.
   376â†’	// In theory, these arrays should have always only one element - the
   377â†’	// controller does not allow more than one attachment. But use array just
   378â†’	// in case...
   379â†’	otherNodes := []types.NodeName{}
   380â†’	otherNodesStr := []string{}
   381â†’	for _, node := range nodes {
   382â†’		if node != volumeToAttach.NodeName {
   383â†’			otherNodes = append(otherNodes, node)
   384â†’			otherNodesStr = append(otherNodesStr, string(node))
   385â†’		}
   386â†’	}
   387â†’
   388â†’	// Get list of pods that use the volume on the other nodes.
   389â†’	pods := rc.desiredStateOfWorld.GetVolumePodsOnNodes(otherNodes, volumeToAttach.VolumeName)
   390â†’	if len(pods) == 0 {
   391â†’		// We did not find any pods that requests the volume. The pod must have been deleted already.
   392â†’		simpleMsg, _ := volumeToAttach.GenerateMsg("Multi-Attach error", "Volume is already exclusively attached to one node and can't be attached to another")
   393â†’		for _, pod := range volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods {
   394â†’			rc.recorder.Eventf(pod, v1.EventTypeWarning, kevents.FailedAttachVolume, simpleMsg)
   395â†’		}
   396â†’		// Log detailed message to system admin
   397â†’		logger.Info("Multi-Attach error: volume is already exclusively attached and can't be attached to another node", "attachedTo", otherNodesStr, "volume", volumeToAttach)
   398â†’		return
   399â†’	}
   400â†’
   401â†’	// There are pods that require the volume and run on another node. Typically
   402â†’	// it's user error, e.g. a ReplicaSet uses a PVC and has >1 replicas. Let
   403â†’	// the user know what pods are blocking the volume.
   404â†’	for _, scheduledPod := range volumeToAttach.ScheduledPods {
   405â†’		// Each scheduledPod must get a custom message. They can run in
   406â†’		// different namespaces and user of a namespace should not see names of
   407â†’		// pods in other namespaces.
   408â†’		localPodNames := []string{} // Names of pods in scheduledPods's namespace
   409â†’		otherPods := 0              // Count of pods in other namespaces
   410â†’		for _, pod := range pods {
   411â†’			if pod.Namespace == scheduledPod.Namespace {
   412â†’				localPodNames = append(localPodNames, pod.Name)
   413â†’			} else {
   414â†’				otherPods++
   415â†’			}
   416â†’		}
   417â†’
   418â†’		var msg string
   419â†’		if len(localPodNames) > 0 {
   420â†’			msg = fmt.Sprintf("Volume is already used by pod(s) %s", strings.Join(localPodNames, ", "))
   421â†’			if otherPods > 0 {
   422â†’				msg = fmt.Sprintf("%s and %d pod(s) in different namespaces", msg, otherPods)
   423â†’			}
   424â†’		} else {
   425â†’			// No local pods, there are pods only in different namespaces.
   426â†’			msg = fmt.Sprintf("Volume is already used by %d pod(s) in different namespaces", otherPods)
   427â†’		}
   428â†’		simpleMsg, _ := volumeToAttach.GenerateMsg("Multi-Attach error", msg)
   429â†’		rc.recorder.Eventf(scheduledPod, v1.EventTypeWarning, kevents.FailedAttachVolume, simpleMsg)
   430â†’	}
   431â†’
   432â†’	// Log all pods for system admin
   433â†’	logger.Info("Multi-Attach error: volume is already used by pods", "pods", klog.KObjSlice(pods), "attachedTo", otherNodesStr, "volume", volumeToAttach)
   434â†’}

    46â†’func Test_RemoveExternalCloudProviderTaint(t *testing.T) {

   112â†’

   305â†’func createKubeconfigFileForRestConfig(restConfig *rest.Config) string {
   306â†’	clusters := make(map[string]*clientcmdapi.Cluster)
   307â†’	clusters["default-cluster"] = &clientcmdapi.Cluster{
   308â†’		Server:                   restConfig.Host,
   309â†’		TLSServerName:            restConfig.ServerName,
   310â†’		CertificateAuthorityData: restConfig.CAData,
   311â†’	}
   312â†’	contexts := make(map[string]*clientcmdapi.Context)
   313â†’	contexts["default-context"] = &clientcmdapi.Context{
   314â†’		Cluster:  "default-cluster",
   315â†’		AuthInfo: "default-user",
   316â†’	}
   317â†’	authinfos := make(map[string]*clientcmdapi.AuthInfo)
   318â†’	authinfos["default-user"] = &clientcmdapi.AuthInfo{
   319â†’		ClientCertificateData: restConfig.CertData,
   320â†’		ClientKeyData:         restConfig.KeyData,
   321â†’		Token:                 restConfig.BearerToken,
   322â†’	}
   323â†’	clientConfig := clientcmdapi.Config{
   324â†’		Kind:           "Config",
   325â†’		APIVersion:     "v1",
   326â†’		Clusters:       clusters,
   327â†’		Contexts:       contexts,
   328â†’		CurrentContext: "default-context",
   329â†’		AuthInfos:      authinfos,
   330â†’	}
   331â†’	kubeConfigFile, _ := os.CreateTemp("", "kubeconfig")
   332â†’	_ = clientcmd.WriteToFile(clientConfig, kubeConfigFile.Name())
   333â†’	return kubeConfigFile.Name()
   334â†’}

   336â†’func makeNode(name string) *v1.Node {
   337â†’	return &v1.Node{
   338â†’		ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   339â†’			Name: name,
   340â†’		},
   341â†’		Spec: v1.NodeSpec{
   342â†’			Taints: []v1.Taint{{
   343â†’				Key:    cloudproviderapi.TaintExternalCloudProvider,
   344â†’				Value:  "true",
   345â†’				Effect: v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule,
   346â†’			}},
   347â†’			Unschedulable: false,
   348â†’		},
   349â†’		Status: v1.NodeStatus{
   350â†’			Conditions: []v1.NodeCondition{
   351â†’				{
   352â†’					Type:              v1.NodeReady,
   353â†’					Status:            v1.ConditionUnknown,
   354â†’					LastHeartbeatTime: metav1.Time{Time: time.Now()},
   355â†’				},
   356â†’			},
   357â†’		},
   358â†’	}
   359â†’}

// Package app implements a server that runs a set of active
// components.  This includes replication controllers, service endpoints and
// nodes.
package app

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"math/rand"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"sort"
	"time"

	"github.com/blang/semver/v4"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
	coordinationv1 "k8s.io/api/coordination/v1"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"

	"k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/config"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/options"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kube-controller-manager/names"
	kubectrlmgrconfig "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/apis/config"
	garbagecollector "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/garbagecollector"
	serviceaccountcontroller "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/serviceaccount"
	kubefeatures "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/serviceaccount"
)

func init() {
	utilruntime.Must(logsapi.AddFeatureGates(utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate))
	utilruntime.Must(metricsfeatures.AddFeatureGates(utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate))
}

	// ControllerStartJitter is the Jitter used when starting controller managers

)

	_, _ = featuregate.DefaultComponentGlobalsRegistry.ComponentGlobalsOrRegister(

1â†’/*
     2â†’Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.
     3â†’
     4â†’Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5â†’you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6â†’You may obtain a copy of the License at
     7â†’
     8â†’    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9â†’
    10â†’Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11â†’distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12â†’WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13â†’See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14â†’limitations under the License.
    15â†’*/
    16â†’
    17â†’package main
    18â†’
    19â†’import (
    20â†’	"flag"
    21â†’	"time"
    22â†’
    23â†’	kubeinformers "k8s.io/client-go/informers"
    24â†’	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
    25â†’	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/clientcmd"
    26â†’	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    27â†’	"k8s.io/sample-controller/pkg/signals"
    28â†’	// Uncomment the following line to load the gcp plugin (only required to authenticate against GKE clusters).
    29â†’	// _ "k8s.io/client-go/plugin/pkg/client/auth/gcp"
    30â†’
    31â†’	clientset "k8s.io/sample-controller/pkg/generated/clientset/versioned"
    32â†’	informers "k8s.io/sample-controller/pkg/generated/informers/externalversions"
    33â†’)
    34â†’
    35â†’var (
    36â†’	masterURL  string
    37â†’	kubeconfig string
    38â†’)
    39â†’
    40â†’func main() {
    41â†’	klog.InitFlags(nil)
    42â†’	flag.Parse()
    43â†’
    44â†’	// set up signals so we handle the shutdown signal gracefully
    45â†’	ctx := signals.SetupSignalHandler()
    46â†’	logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
    47â†’
    48â†’	cfg, err := clientcmd.BuildConfigFromFlags(masterURL, kubeconfig)
    49â†’	if err != nil {
    50â†’		logger.Error(err, "Error building kubeconfig")
    51â†’		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
    52â†’	}
    53â†’
    54â†’	kubeClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(cfg)
    55â†’	if err != nil {
    56â†’		logger.Error(err, "Error building kubernetes clientset")
    57â†’		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
    58â†’	}
    59â†’
    60â†’	exampleClient, err := clientset.NewForConfig(cfg)
    61â†’	if err != nil {
    62â†’		logger.Error(err, "Error building kubernetes clientset")
    63â†’		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
    64â†’	}
    65â†’
    66â†’	kubeInformerFactory := kubeinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(kubeClient, time.Second*30)
    67â†’	exampleInformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(exampleClient, time.Second*30)
    68â†’
    69â†’	controller := NewController(ctx, kubeClient, exampleClient,
    70â†’		kubeInformerFactory.Apps().V1().Deployments(),
    71â†’		exampleInformerFactory.Samplecontroller().V1alpha1().Foos())
    72â†’
    73â†’	// notice that there is no need to run Start methods in a separate goroutine. (i.e. go kubeInformerFactory.Start(ctx.done())
    74â†’	// Start method is non-blocking and runs all registered informers in a dedicated goroutine.
    75â†’	kubeInformerFactory.Start(ctx.Done())
    76â†’	exampleInformerFactory.Start(ctx.Done())
    77â†’
    78â†’	if err = controller.Run(ctx, 2); err != nil {
    79â†’		logger.Error(err, "Error running controller")
    80â†’		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
    81â†’	}
    82â†’}
    83â†’
    84â†’func init() {
    85â†’	flag.StringVar(&kubeconfig, "kubeconfig", "", "Path to a kubeconfig. Only required if out-of-cluster.")
    86â†’	flag.StringVar(&masterURL, "master", "", "The address of the Kubernetes API server. Overrides any value in kubeconfig. Only required if out-of-cluster.")
    87â†’}
    88â†’

1â†’/*
     2â†’Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.
